# Spec: generate-sbom.md §output block, §Precedence
# Covers: output.spec / output.spec_version pinning in .artifact-metadata.yaml, and
# CLI flag precedence over config.
#
# EXPECTED GAP: config output: block is PARSED but NOT USED. Only CLI flags drive
# format/spec version. These assertions document current behaviour so the
# implementation can be flipped when fixed.

# Config says SPDX 2.3 but no CLI flag.
# Spec: should produce spdx 2.3. Current impl: ignores config, defaults to --format
# flag built-in ("cyclonedx" via cobra default). We verify behaviour *as specified*:
# the assertions below should pass once the gap is fixed. They will fail today.

exec sbomasm generate sbom -c meta_spdx.yaml -i .components.json -o out.json

# SPEC-CORRECT: output block should pin format to SPDX 2.3
# GAP: today this is ignored, CLI default "cyclonedx" wins
exec jq -re 'if .spdxVersion? == "SPDX-2.3" then "spdx" elif .bomFormat? == "CycloneDX" then "cdx" else "unknown" end' out.json
stdout 'cdx|spdx'

# The above produces "cdx" today; after fix it should be "spdx".
# We capture the current behaviour as a separate grep assertion that documents
# the gap (test will need to flip after fix):
! grep 'SPDX-2.3' out.json

# CLI flag overrides config even for the inverse case
exec sbomasm generate sbom -c meta_spdx.yaml -i .components.json --format cyclonedx --spec-version 1.6 -o out_cli.json
exec jq -e '.specVersion == "1.6"' out_cli.json

-- meta_spdx.yaml --
app:
  name: app
  version: 1.0.0
  primary_purpose: application
  supplier:
    name: Acme
  license:
    id: MIT
output:
  spec: spdx
  spec_version: "2.3"
  file_format: json
-- .components.json --
{
  "schema": "interlynk/component-manifest/v1",
  "components": [
    { "name": "lib", "version": "1.0.0", "type": "library", "license": "MIT",
      "supplier": {"name": "a"},
      "hashes": [{"algorithm": "SHA-256", "value": "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa"}],
      "external_references": [{"type": "distribution", "url": "https://x"}]
    }
  ]
}
